rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Superior Court To Deposit Surplus Funds
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER CLEAR RECON CORP'S PETITION TO SUPERIOR COURT TO DEPOSIT SURPLUS FUNDS is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. THAI MING CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Judgment Creditors Motion For The Court To Accept Highest Bid For The Sale Of Judgment Debtor Tina Yans Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR THE COURT TO ACCEPT HIGHEST BID FOR SALE OF JUDGMENT DEBTOR TINA YAN'S DWELLING is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice. Notice of Motion filed and served on May 19, 2023 by mail on the judgement debtor purportedly for a hearing on June 15, 2023. The body of the Notice lists the hearing date as "June 17, 2023." Additionally, papers in support of the motion filed and served by mail on May 22, 2023 (Points and Authorities) and May 30, 2023 (Declarations and Request for Judicial Notice) all listing June 15, 2023 as the hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. MARK REID ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 15, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
